test_cases.title: Verify that the user can successfully log time with valid inputs.
preconditions: User is allocated to the project
steps: 1. Log into the Calculus application.
2. Navigate to the Timesheet Entry Page.
3. Select a valid date within the current month.
4. Select a valid work log category from the portfolio manager.
5. Select a valid project from Brac or BracIT Jira.
6. Enter a numeric value greater than zero for hours.
7. Enter a valid work description.
8. Save the entry as Draft.
9. Submit the completed entry.
expected_result: The entry should be created successfully.
The entry should be displayed in the Timesheet Management module.

test_cases.title: Ensure that invalid or missing inputs block submission.
preconditions: User is allocated to the project
steps: 1. Log into the Calculus application.
2. Navigate to the Timesheet Entry Page.
3. Select a valid date within the current month.
4. Leave the hours field blank.
5. Enter a work description.
6. Save the entry as Draft.
7. Attempt to submit the entry.
expected_result: The system should display an error message indicating that hours are mandatory.
The entry should not be saved.

test_cases.title: Validate that hours must be numeric and greater than zero.
preconditions: User is allocated to the project
steps: 1. Log into the Calculus application.
2. Navigate to the Timesheet Entry Page.
3. Select a valid date within the current month.
4. Select a valid work log category from the portfolio manager.
5. Select a valid project from Brac or BracIT Jira.
6. Enter a non-numeric value for hours.
7. Enter a valid work description.
8. Save the entry as Draft.
9. Attempt to submit the entry.
expected_result: The system should display an error message indicating that hours must be a numeric value greater than zero.
The entry should not be saved.

test_cases.title: Confirm that the system allows submitting completed entries.
preconditions: User is allocated to the project
steps: 1. Log into the Calculus application.
2. Navigate to the Timesheet Entry Page.
3. Select a valid date within the current month.
4. Select a valid work log category from the portfolio manager.
5. Select a valid project from Brac or BracIT Jira.
6. Enter a numeric value greater than zero for hours.
7. Enter a valid work description.
8. Save the entry as Draft.
9. Change the status to 'Completed'.
10. Submit the completed entry.
expected_result: The entry should be marked as completed and displayed as such.
The entry should be saved successfully.

test_cases.title: Verify that the system does not allow logging without allocation.
preconditions: User is NOT allocated to the project
steps: 1. Log into the Calculus application.
2. Navigate to the Timesheet Entry Page.
3. Select a valid date within the current month.
4. Select a valid work log category from the portfolio manager.
5. Select a valid project from Brac or BracIT Jira.
6. Enter a numeric value greater than zero for hours.
7. Enter a valid work description.
8. Save the entry as Draft.
9. Attempt to submit the entry.
expected_result: The system should display an error message indicating that the user must be allocated to the project.
The entry should not be saved.

test_cases.title: Validate that the list of Work log category is static and taken from portfolio manager.
preconditions: User is allocated to the project
steps: 1. Log into the Calculus application.
2. Navigate to the Timesheet Entry Page.
3. Select a valid date within the current month.
4. Select a valid work log category from the portfolio manager.
5. Select a valid project from Brac or BracIT Jira.
6. Enter a numeric value greater than zero for hours.
7. Enter a valid work description.
8. Save the entry as Draft.
9. Submit the completed entry.
expected_result: The work log category should be selected from the static list provided by the portfolio manager.
The entry should be created successfully.

test_cases.title: Confirm that project is searchable from both Brac and BracIT jira and shown list must be assigned to the user.
preconditions: User is allocated to the project
steps: 1. Log into the Calculus application.
2. Navigate to the Timesheet Entry Page.
3. Select a valid date within the current month.
4. Search for a project using Brac or BracIT Jira.
5. Verify that the project appears in the list.
6. Verify that the project is assigned to the user.
expected_result: The project should be displayed in the list.
The project should be correctly assigned to the user.

test_cases.title: Verify that the system allows selecting a date beyond the current month.
preconditions: User is allocated to the project
steps: 1. Log into the Calculus application.
2. Navigate to the Timesheet Entry Page.
3. Select a date beyond the current month.
4. Select a valid work log category from the portfolio manager.
5. Select a valid project from Brac or BracIT Jira.
6. Enter a numeric value greater than zero for hours.
7. Enter a valid work description.
8. Save the entry as Draft.
9. Attempt to submit the entry.
expected_result: The system should display an error message indicating that the date must be within the current month.
The entry should not be saved.

test_cases.title: Ensure that the hours field is numeric and greater than zero.
preconditions: User is allocated to the project
steps: 1. Log into the Calculus application.
2. Navigate to the Timesheet Entry Page.
3. Select a valid date within the current month.
4. Select a valid work log category from the portfolio manager.
5. Select a valid project from Brac or BracIT Jira.
6. Enter '0' for hours.
7. Enter a valid work description.
8. Save the entry as Draft.
9. Attempt to submit the entry.
expected_result: The system should display an error message indicating that hours must be greater than zero.
The entry should not be saved.

test_cases.title: Validate that the Work Description field is available.
preconditions: User is allocated to the project
steps: 1. Log into the Calculus application.
2. Navigate to the Timesheet Entry Page.
3. Select a valid date within the current month.
4. Select a valid work log category from the portfolio manager.
5. Select a valid project from Brac or BracIT Jira.
6. Enter a numeric value greater than zero for hours.
7. Enter a valid work description.
8. Save the entry as Draft.
9. Submit the completed entry.
expected_result: The entry should be created successfully.
The work description should be saved with the entry.

test_cases.title: Confirm that the system allows submitting completed entries with zero hours.
preconditions: User is allocated to the project
steps: 1. Log into the Calculus application.
2. Navigate to the Timesheet Entry Page.
3. Select a valid date within the current month.
4. Select a valid work log category from the portfolio manager.
5. Select a valid project from Brac or BracIT Jira.
6. Enter '0' for hours.
7. Enter a valid work description.
8. Save the entry as Draft.
9. Change the status to 'Completed'.
10. Submit the completed entry.
expected_result: The entry should be marked as completed successfully.
The entry should be saved as a completed entry with zero hours.

test_cases.title: Verify that the system handles invalid date formats correctly.
preconditions: User is allocated to the project
steps: 1. Log into the Calculus application.
2. Navigate to the Timesheet Entry Page.
3. Enter an invalid date format (e.g., '2023/10/26').
4. Select a valid work log category from the portfolio manager.
5. Select a valid project from Brac or BracIT Jira.
6. Enter a numeric value greater than zero for hours.
7. Enter a valid work description.
8. Save the entry as Draft.
9. Attempt to submit the entry.
expected_result: The system should display an error message indicating that the date format is invalid.
The entry should not be saved.

test_cases.title: Confirm that the system correctly handles large numeric values for hours.
preconditions: User is allocated to the project
steps: 1. Log into the Calculus application.
2. Navigate to the Timesheet Entry Page.
3. Select a valid date within the current month.
4. Select a valid work log category from the portfolio manager.
5. Select a valid project from Brac or BracIT Jira.
6. Enter a large numeric value (e.g., 999999) for hours.
7. Enter a valid work description.
8. Save the entry as Draft.
9. Submit the completed entry.
expected_result: The entry should be created successfully.
The entry should be saved with the large numeric value for hours.

test_cases.title: Validate that the system correctly handles empty work descriptions.
preconditions: User is allocated to the project
steps: 1. Log into the Calculus application.
2. Navigate to the Timesheet Entry Page.
3. Select a valid date within the current month.
4. Select a valid work log category from the portfolio manager.
5. Select a valid project from Brac or BracIT Jira.
6. Enter a numeric value greater than zero for hours.
7. Leave the work description field blank.
8. Save the entry as Draft.
9. Submit the completed entry.
expected_result: The entry should be created successfully.
The entry should be saved with an empty work description.

test_cases.title: Verify that the system correctly handles special characters in the work description.
preconditions: User is allocated to the project
steps: 1. Log into the Calculus application.
2. Navigate to the Timesheet Entry Page.
3. Select a valid date within the current month.
4. Select a valid work log category from the portfolio manager.
5. Select a valid project from Brac or BracIT Jira.
6. Enter a numeric value greater than zero for hours.
7. Enter a work description containing special characters (e.g., 'This is a test!@#$%^&*()').
8. Save the entry as Draft.
9. Submit the completed entry.
expected_result: The entry should be created successfully.
The entry should be saved with the work description containing special characters.
